metadata.actual_document_title: Principles of Premarket Pathways for Combination Products
metadata.document_type: FDA Guidance for Industry and FDA Staff
metadata.issuing_body: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, Food and Drug Administration, Office of Combination Products
metadata.note: Covers premarket submission pathways for combination products under 21 CFR Part 3

rules.rule_text: Combination products must be assigned to the FDA Center with primary jurisdiction based on the Primary Mode of Action (PMOA) of the combination product.
rules.source_section: Section III – Determining Primary Mode of Action
rules.verbatim_quote: FDA determines which Center has primary jurisdiction for a combination product based on the primary mode of action (PMOA) of the combination product.
exceptions: If PMOA cannot be determined, FDA uses the de novo PMOA process under 21 CFR 3.4(b)

rules.rule_text: A single application is generally the appropriate premarket submission pathway for a combination product, filed with the lead Center based on PMOA.
rules.verbatim_quote: A single application is generally the appropriate premarket submission pathway for a combination product. The application type is determined by the PMOA and is filed with the lead Center.
exceptions: Separate applications may be used when agreed upon by FDA; when marketed separately by different companies; or under other specific circumstances

rules.rule_text: Sponsors must identify a product as a combination product on the applicable FDA submission form when submitting a premarket application.
rules.verbatim_quote: Sponsors should identify their product as a combination product on the applicable FDA form when submitting a premarket application.

rules.rule_text: Sponsors uncertain about the regulatory pathway for a combination product may request a formal Designation (RFD) from the Office of Combination Products to determine the lead Center and appropriate application type.
rules.verbatim_quote: A sponsor may request a formal Designation (RFD) from OCP to obtain FDA's determination of the appropriate center assignment and application type for a combination product.

rules.rule_text: Device-led combination products whose PMOA is attributable to the device component must submit a Premarket Approval Application (PMA) if the device component is or would be classified as Class III.
rules.verbatim_quote: If the combination product has a device PMOA and the device component is or would be classified as Class III, premarket approval (PMA) is required.
exceptions: De Novo classification may be used if the device is novel with low to moderate risk

rules.rule_text: PMA applications for device-led combination products must include valid scientific evidence providing reasonable assurance of safety and effectiveness, including clinical data when required.
rules.verbatim_quote: A PMA must contain valid scientific evidence, including results of clinical studies, demonstrating reasonable assurance of safety and effectiveness of the device component and the combination product as a whole.

rules.rule_text: A 510(k) premarket notification may be used for a device-led combination product only if there is a legally marketed predicate device and the combination product is substantially equivalent in intended use and technological characteristics.
rules.verbatim_quote: A 510(k) can be submitted for a device-led combination product if the device component, and the combination product as a whole, is substantially equivalent to a legally marketed predicate device.

rules.rule_text: A 510(k) cannot be used if the combination product has a new intended use or raises different questions of safety and effectiveness compared to the predicate.
rules.verbatim_quote: A 510(k) cannot be used if the combination product has a new intended use or raises different questions of safety and effectiveness compared to the predicate device.

rules.rule_text: The De Novo classification process must be used when a novel device-led combination product is of low to moderate risk but lacks a valid 510(k) predicate.
rules.verbatim_quote: The De Novo classification process is used to classify a novel device into Class I or Class II when there is no valid predicate and general controls, or general and special controls, are sufficient to provide reasonable assurance of safety and effectiveness.

rules.rule_text: Drug-led combination products whose PMOA is attributable to the drug component must be submitted under a New Drug Application (NDA) or Abbreviated New Drug Application (ANDA), reviewed by CDER.
rules.verbatim_quote: If the combination product has a drug PMOA, the application type is an NDA or ANDA and is reviewed by CDER.

rules.rule_text: ANDA submissions for drug-led combination products must include a demonstration of bioequivalence to the reference listed drug, in addition to meeting device requirements.
rules.verbatim_quote: An ANDA must include a demonstration of bioequivalence to the reference listed drug. In addition, the ANDA must address any applicable device requirements.

rules.rule_text: Biologic-led combination products whose PMOA is attributable to the biological component must be submitted under a Biologics License Application (BLA), reviewed by CBER or CDER.
rules.verbatim_quote: If the combination product has a biologic PMOA, the application type is a BLA and is reviewed by CBER or CDER, depending on the type of biologic.

rules.rule_text: The biosimilar pathway under Section 351(k) of the Public Health Service Act cannot be used for a combination product if the device component design difference causes a clinically meaningful difference in safety, purity, or potency compared to the reference product.
rules.verbatim_quote: The 351(k) pathway is not available if the difference in the device component design causes a clinically meaningful difference in the safety, purity, or potency of the biological product compared to the reference product.

rules.rule_text: Separate applications for a combination product are permissible but require coordination through the Office of Combination Products (OCP) and agreement from each involved Center.
rules.verbatim_quote: Separate applications for a combination product require coordination through OCP and agreement from each Center involved in the review.

rules.rule_text: When a combination product is reviewed under a single application, the lead Center is responsible for overall review coordination and must consult the non-lead Center on aspects of the product within that Center's expertise.
rules.verbatim_quote: The lead Center coordinates the overall review and consults with the non-lead Center on aspects of the combination product within that Center's area of expertise.

rules.rule_text: Sponsors of combination products should engage with FDA early in development through pre-submission meetings to clarify the applicable regulatory pathway, application type, and required studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: Sponsors are encouraged to engage with FDA early in the development process to discuss the applicable regulatory pathway, the type of application required, and the studies that will be needed to support the application.

rules.rule_text: For device-led combination products submitted under PMA, the application must address both the device component (safety and effectiveness) and the drug or biologic component (safety, efficacy, and labeling) in the same submission.
rules.source_section: Section V.A – PMA Content Requirements for Combination Products
rules.verbatim_quote: For a device-led combination product submitted under a PMA, the application must address requirements for both the device component and the constituent drug or biologic component.

rules.rule_text: Annex examples demonstrate that a prefilled syringe is classified as a drug-led combination product and should be submitted under an NDA or BLA rather than a 510(k).
rules.verbatim_quote: A prefilled syringe containing a drug product has a drug PMOA; the appropriate premarket submission is an NDA or BLA filed with the drug or biologic lead Center.

rules.rule_text: A drug-eluting stent is classified as a device-led combination product when the device component provides the primary therapeutic benefit; the application must be submitted as a PMA to CDRH.
rules.verbatim_quote: A drug-eluting stent, where the primary therapeutic benefit is attributable to the device component (the stent), has a device PMOA and must be submitted as a PMA to CDRH.

rules.rule_text: Combination products that are co-packaged (device and drug distributed together but not physically combined) must still be classified and submitted under the appropriate pathway based on PMOA.
rules.source_section: Section II – Definitions and Types of Combination Products
rules.verbatim_quote: Co-packaged combination products (i.e., two or more separate products packaged together in a single package) are subject to the same PMOA determination and premarket submission requirements as other combination products.

rules.rule_text: Cross-labeled combination products (device and drug each labeled for use with the other but distributed separately) are combination products subject to 21 CFR Part 3 assignment.
rules.source_section: Section II – Definitions and Types of Combination Products
rules.verbatim_quote: A product consisting of two or more separate products packaged separately that, according to their labeling, are intended for use only with each other, are a combination product under 21 CFR 3.2(e).

rules.rule_text: When the PMOA of a combination product cannot be determined using the PMOA algorithm, FDA will use a risk-based approach considering the regulatory history of similar products to assign lead Center jurisdiction.
rules.source_section: Section III – PMOA Determination When PMOA Is Not Determinable
rules.verbatim_quote: When the PMOA of a combination product cannot be determined, FDA uses a risk-based approach, including consideration of the regulatory history of similar products, to assign lead Center jurisdiction.

rules.rule_text: All constituent parts of a combination product must comply with the applicable requirements for their product type (device, drug, or biologic), even when reviewed through a single lead Center application.
rules.source_section: Section IV – Regulatory Requirements for Constituent Parts
rules.verbatim_quote: All constituent parts of a combination product must comply with the applicable requirements for that type of product, even when the product is reviewed through a single application.

rules.rule_text: Post-market requirements (including manufacturing, labeling, quality systems, and adverse event reporting) applicable to each constituent part type continue to apply to combination products after approval.
rules.verbatim_quote: Post-market requirements applicable to each constituent part type (e.g., cGMP for drugs, QSR for devices) continue to apply to combination products following approval.

rules.rule_text: Sponsors must submit an IND for clinical studies of a drug-led or biologic-led combination product that involves human subjects and the drug/biologic has not been previously approved.
rules.source_section: Section V.D – IND Requirements for Combination Products
rules.verbatim_quote: An IND is required for clinical studies of a drug-led combination product when the drug component has not been previously approved and involves human subjects.
exceptions: Studies of previously approved drug components combined with a new device may use IDE for the device component

rules.rule_text: An Investigational Device Exemption (IDE) is required for significant risk device-led combination product clinical studies unless the product is otherwise exempt.
rules.verbatim_quote: An IDE is required for clinical studies of a significant risk device-led combination product unless the study is exempt under 21 CFR 812.2(c).
exceptions: Non-significant risk device studies; studies of approved devices; other exemptions under 21 CFR 812.2(c)

rules.rule_text: OCP intercenter agreements and assignment letters govern the division of review responsibilities between Centers and are binding on the review process for the assigned combination product.
rules.verbatim_quote: Intercenter agreements (ICAs) and assignment letters from OCP govern the division of responsibilities between Centers and are binding on the review process.

rules.rule_text: For 510(k) submissions for device-led combination products, substantial equivalence must be demonstrated for both the device component and for any drug or biologic constituent parts.
rules.source_section: Section V.B – 510(k) Substantial Equivalence for Combination Products
rules.verbatim_quote: Substantial equivalence must be demonstrated for the combination product as a whole, including for any drug or biologic constituent part, when submitting a 510(k) for a device-led combination product.

rules.rule_text: Sponsors seeking De Novo classification for a novel device-led combination product must submit a De Novo request with proposed special controls sufficient to provide reasonable assurance of safety and effectiveness.
rules.verbatim_quote: A De Novo request must include proposed special controls that, together with general controls, are sufficient to provide reasonable assurance of safety and effectiveness of the device-led combination product.

rules.rule_text: The labeling of a combination product must comply with labeling requirements applicable to each constituent part, including both drug and device labeling requirements.
rules.source_section: Section VI – Labeling Requirements for Combination Products
rules.verbatim_quote: The labeling of a combination product must comply with all labeling requirements applicable to each constituent part, including applicable drug labeling requirements and device labeling requirements.
